seoTitle: Pleasant Park vs Plympton Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pleasant Park and Plympton Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pleasant Park vs Plympton Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pleasant Park and Plympton Park.
heading: Pleasant Park vs Plympton Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pleasant Park, South Australia and Plympton Park, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pleasant Park has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Plympton Park (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pleasant Park or Plympton Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pleasant Park has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Plympton Park scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pleasant Park scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Plympton Park scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pleasant Park or Plympton Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pleasant Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
